tasks.title: Auto-merge passing PRs (#27-#34)

tasks.title: Track velocity per version → reports/velocity.jsonl
tasks.description: After each version completes, append {version, tasks_done, avg_quality, elapsed_s, timestamp} to reports/velocity.jsonl. Read by dashboard benchmarker panel.

result.output:
import json
from datetime import datetime

# Function to calculate average quality (assuming a simple average for demonstration)
def calculate_avg_quality(tasks):
    return sum(task['quality'] for task in tasks) / len(tasks)

# Function to track velocity per version and append data to reports/velocity.jsonl
def track_velocity(version, tasks_done, avg_quality, elapsed_s):
    timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
    data = {
        "version": version,
        "tasks_done": tasks_done,
        "avg_quality": avg_quality,
        "elapsed_s": elapsed_s,
        "timestamp": timestamp
    }
    
    with open('reports/velocity.jsonl', 'a') as file:
        json.dump(data, file)
        file.write('\n')

# Example usage and assertions to verify correctness
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Simulated data for testing
    version = "v1.0"
    tasks_done = 5
    avg_quality = calculate_avg_quality([{'quality': 8}, {'quality': 9}, {'quality': 7}])
    elapsed_s = 3600

    # Call the function to track velocity
    track_velocity(version, tasks_done, avg_quality, elapsed_s)

    # Read back from the file and verify correctness
    with open('reports/velocity.jsonl', 'r') as file:
        lines = file.readlines()
        assert len(lines) == 1, "Expected one line in the file"
        data = json.loads(lines[0])
        assert data['version'] == version, f"Expected version {version}, got {data['version']}"
        assert data['tasks_done'] == tasks_done, f"Expected tasks done {tasks_done}, got {data['tasks_done']}"
        assert data['avg_quality'] == avg_quality, f"Expected average quality {avg_quality}, got {data['avg_quality']}"
        assert data['elapsed_s'] == elapsed_s, f"Expected elapsed time {elapsed_s} seconds, got {data['elapsed_s']}"
        assert 'timestamp' in data, "Timestamp should be present"

projects.name: Nexus Dashboard
projects.description: Local runtime dashboard. Never goes down. Stale alert fires at 10s. All work handled by frontend_agent only — never Claude main.

epics.title: Never-Down Guarantee
epics.description: Dashboard must always show data. Stale banner fires after 10s. Updater auto-restarts on crash.

tasks.title: Keep state.json updated every 2s (live_state_updater.py daemon)
tasks.description: Run python3 dashboard/live_state_updater.py as background daemon. It must write state.json every 2s with live agent status. Verify: tail -f dashboard/state.json shows updates.

result.output:
import json
import time
from threading import Thread

def update_state():
    while True:
        # Simulate getting live agent status (this should be replaced with actual logic)
        agent_status = {
            "agent1": "active",
            "agent2": "inactive"
        }
        
        # Write the state to state.json
        with open('/Users/jimmymalhan/stacky-os/state.json', 'w') as f:
            json.dump(agent_status, f, indent=4)
        
        # Sleep for 2 seconds before updating again
        time.sleep(2)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    daemon_thread = Thread(target=update_state)
    daemon_thread.daemon = True
    daemon_thread.start()

tasks.title: Cron: restart live_state_updater.py on crash (every 1 min)

tasks.title: Stale-data test — verify banner appears after 15s server kill

tasks.title: Cron: keep live_state_updater.py always running
tasks.description: Add cron: */1 * * * * pgrep -f live_state_updater.py || python3 /Users/jimmymalhan/Documents/local-agent-runtime/local-agents/dashboard/live_state_updater.py &. Test: kill the process, verify restart within 60s. Prevents dashboard empty values.

result.output:
import os
import subprocess
import time

def setup_cron_job():
    cron_entry = "*/1 * * * * pgrep -f live_state_updater.py || python3 /Users/jimmymalhan/Documents/local-agent-runtime/local-agents/dashboard/live_state_updater.py &"
    with open("/etc/crontab", "a") as crontab_file:
        crontab_file.write(cron_entry + "\n")

def test_restart():
    # Kill the live_state_updater process
    subprocess.run(["pkill", "-f", "live_state_updater.py"])
    
    start_time = time.time()
    while True:
        if subprocess.run(["pgrep", "-f", "live_state_updater.py"]).returncode == 0:
            break
        if time.time() - start_time > 60:
            raise AssertionError("Process did not restart within 60 seconds")
        time.sleep(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    setup_cron_job()
    test_restart()

---

import subprocess
import time

def check_and_restart_live_state_updater():
    process_name = "live_state_updater.py"
    command = f"pgrep -f {process_name}"
    
    # Check if the process is running
    result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"{process_name} is not running. Restarting...")
        restart_command = f"python3 /Users/jimmymalhan/Documents/local-agent-runtime/local-agents/dashboard/{process_name} &"
        subprocess.run(restart_command, shell=True)
        print(f"{process_name} has been restarted.")
    else:
        print(f"{process_name} is running.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Test assertions
    assert check_and_restart_live_state_updater() is None  # No-op if process is running
    
    # Simulate killing the process and verify restart
    subprocess.run("pkill -f live_state_updater.py", shell=True)
    time.sleep(10)  # Wait for the cron job to run
    check_and_restart_live_state_updater()

tasks.title: Regression test: live_state_updater must write benchmark_scores and token_usage on every tick
tasks.description: Write tests/test_live_state_updater.py. After each tick(), assert: benchmark_scores has >= 1 version key, token_usage has claude_tokens field, ts is within 5s of now. Run on every loop start.

tasks.title: state_writer.update_agent() must write quality field
tasks.description: update_agent() in dashboard/state_writer.py writes status/task/elapsed_s but NOT quality. Add quality=0 default param. All agents must call update_agent(name, status, task, quality=result_quality) after each run. Without this, agent cards show blank quality score on dashboard.

tasks.title: sub_agents model field must be populated — model chips show blank
tasks.description: state_writer.update_sub_agents() accepts model but executor sub_agents write model="" (empty string). Fix: SubAgentPool must pass model=AGENT_META["model"] (e.g. "qwen2.5-coder:7b") for each worker dict. Without this, model chip on sub-agent cards shows blank.

tasks.title: live_state_updater must synthesize recent_tasks from v*_compare.jsonl
tasks.description: state.json recent_tasks is null because live_state_updater never reads it. Fix: in tick(), read last 10 entries across all v*_compare.jsonl files, build [{task_id, title, category, agent_used, status, local_quality}] and write to state["recent_tasks"]. This populates the Tasks tab activity feed.

tasks.title: orchestrator must call state_writer.update_version_changelog() after each version
tasks.description: version_changelog:{} because orchestrator/main.py never calls update_version_changelog(). Fix: after each version loop completes, call update_version_changelog(version, changes=["tasks done", "avg quality", "win rate"]) from dashboard.state_writer. This populates CEO tab changelog section.

tasks.title: agents must call state_writer.add_research_finding() when they discover patterns
tasks.description: research_feed:[] because no agent calls add_research_finding(). Fix: researcher.py and benchmarker.py must call add_research_finding(finding, source, confidence) after each analysis. Also add synthetic entries from compare files in live_state_updater when win_rate changes. Populates Logs tab.

tasks.title: live_state_updater must write projects task counts from projects.json
tasks.description: Dashboard Projects tab shows empty epics/task counts because live_state_updater never reads projects/projects.json. Fix: in tick(), read projects.json, compute per-project stats (total/done/in_progress/blocked), write to state["projects_summary"]. Server already has /api/projects endpoint.

tasks.title: macOS cron 'Operation not permitted' blocker — cron cannot execute watchdog.sh, rescue_orchestrator.sh, auto_merge_pr.sh because Terminal does not have Full Disk Access. Investigate launchd as alternative to cron, or document exact steps for user to grant FDA in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access. Until fixed, all auto-restart and rescue automation is broken.
tasks.description: macOS cron 'Operation not permitted' blocker — cron cannot execute watchdog.sh, rescue_orchestrator.sh, auto_merge_pr.sh because Terminal does not have Full Disk Access. Investigate launchd as alternative to cron, or document exact steps for user to grant FDA in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access. Until fixed, all auto-restart and rescue automation is broken.

tasks.title: continuous_loop.py cannot persist auto-generated tasks: '[Errno 2] No such file or directory: local-agents/projects/all/tasks.json'. Loop is running but burning CPU in error cycle. Fix: create the missing directory/file path OR update continuous_loop.py to read tasks from orchestrator.task_intake queue instead of projects/all/tasks.json. See /tmp/nexus-loop.log. Acceptance: loop picks up real tasks from task_intake queue without path errors.
